description: §65.2.4 — `button {}` and `a {}` both set `color`, but an element has exactly one tag name, so they are PROVABLY DISJOINT (no element can match both). No overlap → no diagnostic. This proves the checker does not false-fire on two same-property rules that simply cannot co-target an element.
rationale: §65.2.4 decidable core: `tag × tag` with DIFFERENT tags is provably disjoint (an element has exactly one tag name). Two rules that cannot share any element are not a conflict; neither the hard nor the soft diagnostic fires.
